title.zh: 卷三十七 季布欒布田叔傳
title.en: Volume 37: Ji Bu, Luan Bu and Tian Shu
title.raw: 卷三十七 季布欒布田叔傳 Volume 37: Ji Bu, Luan Bu and Tian Shu

sentences.zh: 季布，楚人也，為任俠有名。

translations.literal: Ji Bu, a man of Chu, as one who practiced knight-errantry had a name.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu was a native of Chu and was widely known as a knight-errant.

sentences.zh: 項籍使將兵，數窘漢王。

translations.literal: Xiang Ji sent him to command troops; repeatedly embarrassed the King of Han.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu sent him to lead troops, and time and again he drove the King of Han into desperate straits.

sentences.zh: 項籍滅，高祖購求布千金，敢有舍匿，罪三族。

translations.literal: Xiang Ji destroyed; Gaozu offered a thousand in gold to seek Bu; whoever dared harbor or conceal him, crime to three clans.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu fell, Gaozu offered a thousand catties of gold for Ji Bu's capture, and anyone who dared shelter or hide him would be punished with extermination of three generations of his kin.

sentences.zh: 布匿濮陽周氏，周氏曰：「漢求將軍急，跡且至臣家，能聽臣，臣敢進計；

translations.literal: Bu hid with the Zhou clan of Puyang. The Zhou said: "The Han seeks the general urgently; tracks will soon reach my house. If you can heed your subject, your subject dares advance a plan;"
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu took refuge with the Zhou family of Puyang. The head of the family said, "The Han court is hunting you with great urgency, and their agents will soon trace you here. If you will listen to me, I venture to propose a plan;"

sentences.zh: 即否，願先自剄。」

translations.literal: If not, I wish first myself to cut the throat.
translations.idiomatic: If not, I would rather cut my own throat first.

sentences.zh: 布許之。

translations.literal: Bu assented to it.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu agreed.

sentences.zh: 乃髡鉗布，衣褐，置廣柳車中，并與其家僮數十人，之魯朱家所賣之。

translations.literal: Then shaved-head and cangue Bu, clothed him in coarse cloth, placed in a broad-willow cart, together with several tens of his household servants, went to Lu to Zhu Jia's place and sold him.
translations.idiomatic: They shaved Ji Bu's head, fitted him with an iron collar, dressed him in a laborer's garb, and stowed him in a covered funeral cart along with several dozen household retainers sold as bondservants, then took the party to Lu and sold them to Zhu Jia.

sentences.zh: 朱家心知其季布也，買置田舍。

translations.literal: Zhu Jia's heart knew him to be Ji Bu; bought and placed on fields and houses.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Jia knew at heart that the man was Ji Bu; he purchased him and settled him on a farm.

sentences.zh: 乃之雒陽見汝陰侯滕公，說曰：「季布何罪？

translations.literal: Then went to Luoyang, saw Marquis of Ru-yin Lord Teng, persuaded saying: "What crime has Ji Bu?"
translations.idiomatic: He then traveled to Luoyang to see the Marquis of Ru-yin, Lord Teng (Xiahou Ying), and urged him: "What crime has Ji Bu committed?"

sentences.zh: 臣各為其主用，職耳。

translations.literal: Ministers each for their lord were employed; duty only.
translations.idiomatic: Each minister served the lord he owed allegiance to; that was simply his duty.

sentences.zh: 項氏臣豈可盡誅邪？

translations.literal: Xiang clan's ministers how can all be executed?
translations.idiomatic: Surely you cannot put every minister who served the Xiangs to the sword?

sentences.zh: 今上始得天下，而以私怨求一人，何示不廣也！

translations.literal: Now the sovereign has just obtained the realm, yet by private resentment seeks one man—how show not broad!
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven has only just won the empire; to hunt one man out of private spite shows how narrow his magnanimity would appear!

sentences.zh: 且以季布之賢，漢求之急如此，此不北走胡，南走越耳。

translations.literal: Moreover by Ji Bu's worthiness, Han's seeking him urgently thus—this is not north flee Hu, south flee Yue only.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, a man of Ji Bu's caliber, pressed this hard by the Han, will flee north to the Hu or south to Yue—that is all there is to it.

sentences.zh: 夫忌壯士以資敵國，此伍子胥所以鞭荊平之墓也。

translations.literal: As for envying a stalwart to supply an enemy state—this is why Wu Zixu whipped King Ping of Jing's tomb.
translations.idiomatic: To drive a brave man into the arms of a rival power out of jealousy is the very reason Wu Zixu lashed the grave of King Ping of Chu.

sentences.zh: 君何不從容為上言之？」

translations.literal: Why do you not at ease for the sovereign speak of it?
translations.idiomatic: Why do you not find a suitable moment and put this to the sovereign for him?

sentences.zh: 滕公心知朱家大俠，意布匿其所，乃許諾。

translations.literal: Lord Teng's heart knew Zhu Jia was a great knight-errant; suspected Bu hid at his place; then promised.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Teng knew Zhu Jia for a great knight-errant and guessed that Ji Bu lay hidden with him; he gave his word.

sentences.zh: 侍間，果言如朱家指。

translations.literal: Waiting for an interval, indeed spoke as Zhu Jia indicated.
translations.idiomatic: When an opportunity arose, he spoke to the emperor exactly as Zhu Jia had directed.

sentences.zh: 上乃赦布。

translations.literal: The sovereign then pardoned Bu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon pardoned Ji Bu.

sentences.zh: 當是時，諸公皆多布能摧剛為柔，朱家亦以此名聞當世。

translations.literal: At this time, the great men all praised Bu able to grind hard into soft; Zhu Jia also by this had name heard in that age.
translations.idiomatic: At that time eminent men alike admired Ji Bu for turning inflexible strength into yielding survival, and Zhu Jia's reputation spread through the age for the same deed.

sentences.zh: 布召見，謝，拜郎中。

translations.literal: Bu summoned and seen, thanked, appointed Gentleman of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu was summoned into audience, expressed his gratitude, and was appointed Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 孝惠時，為中郎將。

translations.literal: In Emperor Xiaohui's time, served as General of the Gentlemen of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaohui he served as General of the Gentlemen of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 單于嘗為書嫚呂太后，太后怒，召諸將議之。

translations.literal: The Chanyu once made a letter insulting Empress Dowager Lü; the empress dowager angry, summoned the generals to deliberate on it.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu had once sent a letter that insulted Empress Dowager Lü; enraged, she called in the generals to discuss a response.

sentences.zh: 上將軍樊噲曰：「臣願得十萬眾，橫行匈奴中。」

translations.literal: Senior General Fan Kuai said: "Your servant wishes to obtain a hundred thousand host, rampage through the Xiongnu middle."
translations.idiomatic: Senior General Fan Kuai declared, "I ask for a hundred thousand men to sweep through the heart of the Xiongnu domain."

sentences.zh: 諸將皆阿呂太后，以噲言為然。

translations.literal: The generals all fawned on Empress Dowager Lü, took Kuai's words as right.
translations.idiomatic: The generals all curried favor with Empress Dowager Lü and agreed that Fan Kuai was right.

sentences.zh: 布曰：「樊噲可斬也。

translations.literal: Bu said: "Fan Kuai can be beheaded."
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu said, "Fan Kuai deserves execution."

sentences.zh: 夫以高帝兵三十餘萬，困於平城，噲時亦在其中。

translations.literal: As for with Gaodi's troops thirty-odd myriad, trapped at Pingcheng—Kuai at that time also was within them.
translations.idiomatic: Gaodi led more than three hundred thousand men and was still trapped at Pingcheng, and Fan Kuai was in that army.

sentences.zh: 今噲奈何以十萬眾橫行匈奴中，面謾！

translations.literal: Now how can Kuai with a hundred thousand host rampage through Xiongnu middle—face to deceive!
translations.idiomatic: How dare Fan Kuai now claim he can ride roughshod over the Xiongnu with a mere hundred thousand—he insults us to our faces!

sentences.zh: 且秦以事胡，陳勝等起。

translations.literal: Moreover Qin by serving the Hu, Chen Sheng and others rose.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, when Qin exhausted itself on the northern tribes, Chen Sheng and his like rose in rebellion.

sentences.zh: 今瘡痍未瘳，噲又面諛，欲搖動天下。」

translations.literal: Now wounds not healed; Kuai again face flatters, wishes to shake move the realm.
translations.idiomatic: The wounds of war are not yet healed, and Fan Kuai flatters you to your face in a bid to unsettle the whole realm.

sentences.zh: 是時殿上皆恐，太后罷朝，遂不復議擊匈奴事。

translations.literal: At this time on the hall all feared; empress dowager dismissed court; then no more deliberated striking Xiongnu affairs.
translations.idiomatic: A chill fell over everyone in the hall; the empress dowager ended the audience, and the court never again debated attacking the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 布為河東守。

translations.literal: Bu was Hedong governor.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu served as governor of Hedong.

sentences.zh: 孝文時，人有言其賢，召欲以為御史大夫。

translations.literal: In Emperor Xiaowen's time, someone spoke of his worthiness; summoned, wished to make him Grandee Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen, someone praised his abilities, and the emperor summoned him intending to appoint him Grandee Secretary.

sentences.zh: 人又言其勇，使酒難近。

translations.literal: Someone again spoke of his bravery, wine-driven hard to approach.
translations.idiomatic: Others spoke of his courage but added that he was unruly when drunk and difficult to work with.

sentences.zh: 至，留邸一月，見罷。

translations.literal: Arrived, lodged in guesthouse one month, audience dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When he arrived, he was kept at the official lodging for a month and then dismissed without appointment.

sentences.zh: 布進曰：「臣待罪河東，陛下無故召臣，此人必有以臣欺陛下者。

translations.literal: Bu advanced saying: "Your servant awaits punishment at Hedong; Your Majesty without reason summoned your servant—this person must have something by which to deceive Your Majesty with your servant."
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu stepped forward and said, "I stand awaiting judgment in Hedong; Your Majesty summoned me without cause—someone must have misrepresented me to you."

sentences.zh: 今臣至，無所受事，罷去，此人必有毀臣者。

translations.literal: Now your servant arrived, received no affairs, dismissed and sent away—this person must have one who slanders your servant.
translations.idiomatic: Now that I have come and been given no assignment but sent away, someone must have slandered me.

sentences.zh: 夫陛下以一人譽召臣，一人毀去臣，臣恐天下有識者聞之，有以窺陛下。」

translations.literal: As for Your Majesty by one man's praise summoned your servant, by one man's slander dismissed your servant—your servant fears the realm's knowing ones hearing it will have means to spy Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: If a single man's praise can bring me to court and a single man's blame can send me away, I fear that discerning men everywhere will draw their own conclusions about Your Majesty's judgment.

sentences.zh: 上默然，慚曰：「河東吾股肱郡，故特召君耳。」

translations.literal: The sovereign silent, ashamed said: "Hedong is my thigh-arm province; therefore specially summoned you only."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was silent, then said with embarrassment, "Hedong is a pillar of the realm; that is why I called you here in particular."

sentences.zh: 布之官。

translations.literal: Bu went to his office.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu returned to his post.

sentences.zh: 辯士曹丘生數招權顧金錢，事貴人趙談等，與竇長君善。

translations.literal: Sophist Cao Qiusheng repeatedly summoned power looked to gold and cash, served noble men Zhao Tan and others, with Dou Changjun was good.
translations.idiomatic: The rhetorician Cao Qiusheng often traded on influence for money, cultivated men such as the favorite Zhao Tan, and was on close terms with Dou Changjun.

sentences.zh: 布聞，寄書諫長君曰：「吾聞曹丘生非長者，勿與通。」

translations.literal: Bu heard, sent letter remonstrating Changjun saying: "I have heard Cao Qiusheng is not an elder-worthy; do not communicate with him."
translations.idiomatic: When Ji Bu heard of it, he sent Dou Changjun a letter of warning: "I have heard that Cao Qiusheng is no man of honor; have nothing to do with him."

sentences.zh: 及曹丘生歸，欲得書請布。

translations.literal: When Cao Qiusheng returned, wished to obtain a letter to request Bu.
translations.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng came home, he wanted a letter of introduction so he could call on Ji Bu.

sentences.zh: 竇長君曰：「季將軍不說足下，足下無往。」

translations.literal: Dou Changjun said: "General Ji is not pleased with you; you, do not go."
translations.idiomatic: Dou Changjun said, "General Ji dislikes you; you had better not go."

sentences.zh: 固請書，遂行。

translations.literal: Firmly requested letter; then went.
translations.idiomatic: Cao pressed for the letter until he got it, then set out.

sentences.zh: 使人先發書，布果大怒，待曹丘。

translations.literal: Sent man first to deliver letter; Bu indeed greatly angry, awaited Cao Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: He sent someone ahead with the letter; Ji Bu was furious and waited for Cao Qiusheng in a rage.

sentences.zh: 曹丘至，則揖布曰：「楚人諺曰『得黃金百，不如得季布諾』，足下何以得此聲梁楚之間哉？

translations.literal: Cao Qiu arrived, then bowed to Bu saying: "Chu people proverb says 'obtain yellow gold hundred, not like obtain Ji Bu's promise'—how did you obtain this reputation between Liang and Chu?"
translations.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng arrived, he bowed to Ji Bu and said, "The people of Chu have a saying: 'A hundred catties of gold are not worth Ji Bu's word.' How did you win such a name across Liang and Chu?"

sentences.zh: 且僕與足下俱楚人，使僕游揚足下名於天下，顧不美乎？

translations.literal: Moreover your servant and you both Chu men; if your servant roam-extol your foot's name in the realm—look, is it not beautiful?
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, you and I are both men of Chu; if I spread your reputation through the empire, would that not be splendid?

sentences.zh: 何足下距僕之深也！」

translations.literal: Why you reject your servant so deeply!
translations.idiomatic: Why do you keep me at such a distance!

sentences.zh: 布乃大說。

translations.literal: Bu then greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu was greatly pleased.

sentences.zh: 引入，留數月，為上客，厚送之。

translations.literal: Led him in, retained several months, made upper guest, thickly sent him off.
translations.idiomatic: He brought him inside, kept him for several months as an honored guest, and sent him away with rich gifts.

sentences.zh: 布名所以益聞者，曹丘揚之也。

translations.literal: Bu's name the reason it increased heard—Cao Qiu extolled it.
translations.idiomatic: The reason Ji Bu's fame grew still wider was that Cao Qiusheng proclaimed it.

sentences.zh: 布弟季心氣蓋關中，遇人恭謹，為任俠，方數千里，士爭為死。

translations.literal: Bu's younger brother Ji Xin spirit covered Guanzhong; meeting people respectful cautious; as knight-errant, region several thousand li, knights vied to die for him.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu's younger brother Ji Xin's daring overshadowed the Guanzhong region; though courteous and restrained in personal dealings, as a knight-errant his reputation spread for thousands of li, and fighting men vied to die for him.

sentences.zh: 嘗殺人，亡吳，從爰絲匿，長事爰絲，弟畜灌夫、籍福之屬。

translations.literal: Once killed a man, fled to Wu, followed Yuan Si hiding, elder-served Yuan Si, younger-treated Guan Fu, Ji Fu's sort.
translations.idiomatic: He once killed a man and fled to Wu, where he took refuge with Yuan Ang and served him with the deference due an elder, while treating Guan Fu, Ji Fu, and their circle as younger companions.

sentences.zh: 嘗為中司馬，中尉郅都不敢加。

translations.literal: Once was central marshal; Commandant Zhi Du did not dare apply pressure.
translations.idiomatic: He had served as a marshal in the central command; even Commandant Zhi Du did not dare cross him.

sentences.zh: 少年多時時竊借其名以行。

translations.literal: Youths many often secretly borrowed his name to act.
translations.idiomatic: Young men often quietly traded on his name to get their way.

sentences.zh: 當是時，季心以勇，布以諾，聞關中。

translations.literal: At this time, Ji Xin by courage, Bu by promise, heard in Guanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: In those days Ji Xin was famed in Guanzhong for his bravery and Ji Bu for keeping his word.

sentences.zh: 布母弟丁公，為項羽將，逐窘高祖彭城西。

translations.literal: Bu's mother's younger brother Lord Ding, was Xiang Yu's general, pursued embarrassed Gaozu west of Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu's maternal uncle Lord Ding was a general under Xiang Yu and harried Gaozu into a tight corner west of Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 短兵接，漢王急，顧謂丁公曰：「兩賢豈相厄哉！」

translations.literal: Short weapons met; King of Han urgent, turned told Lord Ding: "Two worthies how mutually press!"
translations.idiomatic: At close quarters the King of Han was desperate; turning to Lord Ding he cried, "Surely two able men need not ruin each other!"

sentences.zh: 丁公引兵而還。

translations.literal: Lord Ding led troops and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Ding withdrew his troops and turned back.

sentences.zh: 及項王滅，丁公謁見高祖，以丁公徇軍中，曰：「丁公為項王臣不忠，使項王失天下者也。」

translations.literal: When King Xiang destroyed, Lord Ding audience-saw Gaozu; with Lord Ding displayed in army middle, said: "Lord Ding as King Xiang's minister not loyal—he who made King Xiang lose the realm."
translations.idiomatic: After King Xiang fell, Lord Ding came to pay his respects to Gaozu; Gaozu had him paraded before the army and declared, "Lord Ding was a disloyal minister to King Xiang and was the man who cost him the empire."

sentences.zh: 遂斬之，曰：「使後為人臣無傚丁公也！」

translations.literal: Then beheaded him, said: "Make later those who are ministers not imitate Lord Ding!"
translations.idiomatic: He had him executed, saying, "Let no minister hereafter take Lord Ding as his model!"

sentences.zh: 欒布，梁人也。

translations.literal: Luan Bu, Liang person.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bu was a native of Liang.

sentences.zh: 彭越為家人時，嘗與布游，窮困，賣庸於齊，為酒家保。

translations.literal: When Peng Yue was commoner, once with Bu roamed; poor trapped, sold hire-labor in Qi, was wine-house bondsman.
translations.idiomatic: When Peng Yue was still a commoner, he had roamed with Luan Bu; in destitution he hired out his labor in Qi and worked as a bondservant in a tavern.

sentences.zh: 數歲別去，而布為人所略，賣為奴於燕。

translations.literal: Several years parted and left, while Bu by people was abducted, sold as slave in Yan.
translations.idiomatic: After some years they parted, and Luan Bu was kidnapped and sold into slavery in Yan.

sentences.zh: 為其主家報仇，燕將臧荼舉以為都尉。

translations.literal: For his master's household avenged grudge; Yan general Zang Tu raised him to be commandant.
translations.idiomatic: He avenged a wrong for his master's family, whereupon the Yan general Zang Tu promoted him to commandant.

sentences.zh: 荼為燕王，布為將。

translations.literal: Tu became Yan king; Bu was general.
translations.idiomatic: When Zang Tu became King of Yan, Luan Bu served as his general.

sentences.zh: 及荼反，漢擊燕，虜布。

translations.literal: When Tu rebelled, Han struck Yan, captured Bu.
translations.idiomatic: When Zang Tu rebelled, the Han attacked Yan and took Luan Bu prisoner.

sentences.zh: 梁王彭越聞之，乃言上，請贖布為梁大夫。

translations.literal: King of Liang Peng Yue heard it, then spoke to the sovereign, requested ransom Bu to be Liang grandee.
translations.idiomatic: King Peng Yue of Liang heard of it and petitioned the emperor to ransom Luan Bu and appoint him a grandee of Liang.

sentences.zh: 使於齊，未反，漢召彭越責以謀反，夷三族，梟首雒陽，下詔有收視者輒捕之。

translations.literal: Sent on mission to Qi, not yet returned; Han summoned Peng Yue reproached with plotting rebellion, exterminated three clans, displayed head Luoyang, issued edict whoever collected viewed immediately arrest.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bu was on a mission to Qi and had not returned when the Han summoned Peng Yue, charged him with plotting rebellion, wiped out three clans of his kin, exposed his head in Luoyang, and issued an edict that anyone who dared mourn or bury him would be arrested at once.

sentences.zh: 布還，奏事彭越頭下，祠而哭之。

translations.literal: Bu returned, reported affairs beneath Peng Yue's head, sacrificed and wept for him.
translations.idiomatic: When Luan Bu returned, he presented his report beneath Peng Yue's severed head, then offered sacrifice and wept for him.

sentences.zh: 吏捕以聞。

translations.literal: Officials arrested and reported.
translations.idiomatic: The clerks seized him and reported the matter.

sentences.zh: 上召布罵曰：「若與彭越反邪？

translations.literal: The sovereign summoned Bu and cursed saying: "You with Peng Yue rebelled?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Luan Bu and shouted, "Were you in rebellion with Peng Yue?"

sentences.zh: 吾禁人勿收，若獨祠而哭之，與反明矣。

translations.literal: I forbade people not to collect; you alone sacrificed and wept for him—with rebel clear.
translations.idiomatic: I forbade anyone to claim his body; you alone mourned him with sacrifice and tears—your guilt is as plain as if you had risen in arms.

sentences.zh: 趣亨之。」

translations.literal: Hurry boil him.
translations.idiomatic: Take him away and boil him alive.

sentences.zh: 方提趨湯，顧曰：「願一言而死。」

translations.literal: Just as raised hurrying to cauldron, turned saying: "Wish one word then die."
translations.idiomatic: As they were dragging him toward the cauldron, he looked back and said, "Let me speak one word before I die."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「何言？」

translations.literal: The sovereign said: "What words?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "What is it?"

sentences.zh: 布曰：「方上之困彭城，敗滎陽、成皋間，項王所以不能遂西，徒以彭王居梁地，與漢合從苦楚也。

translations.literal: Bu said: "When the sovereign was trapped at Pengcheng, defeated between Xingyang and Chenggao, the reason King Xiang could not succeed west was only because Peng King dwelt in Liang territory, with Han joined vertical suffering Chu."
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bu said, "When Your Majesty was pinned down at Pengcheng and driven from Xingyang to Chenggao, the reason King Xiang could not push west was solely that the King of Peng held Liang and allied with Han to exhaust Chu."

sentences.zh: 當是之時，彭王壹顧，與楚則漢破，與漢則楚破。

translations.literal: At this time, if Peng King one glance—with Chu then Han broken; with Han then Chu broken.
translations.idiomatic: In those days a single shift of the King of Peng's weight—if he had thrown in with Chu, Han would have shattered; if with Han, Chu would have shattered.

sentences.zh: 且垓下之會，微彭王，項氏不亡。

translations.literal: Moreover at Gaixia's assembly, without Peng King, Xiang clan would not perish.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, at the battle of Gaixia, had it not been for the King of Peng, the house of Xiang would not have fallen.

sentences.zh: 天下已定，彭王剖符受封，亦欲傳之萬世。

translations.literal: The realm already settled, Peng King split tally received enfeoffment, also wished to transmit it ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: Once the realm was settled, the King of Peng received his tally and fief and hoped to hand them down for ages.

sentences.zh: 今漢壹徵兵於梁，彭王病不行，而疑以為反。

translations.literal: Now Han once called troops from Liang; Peng King sick did not go, yet suspected and deemed rebel.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Han issued one levy in Liang; the king was ill and could not march, and on that slender ground he was suspected of treason.

sentences.zh: 反形未見，以苛細誅之，臣恐功臣人人自危也。

translations.literal: Rebel form not yet seen; by harsh fine executed him—your servant fears merit ministers each man self-will fear.
translations.idiomatic: No sign of rebellion had appeared; to put him to death on petty charges makes me fear that every meritorious minister will begin to doubt his own safety.

sentences.zh: 今彭王已死，臣生不如死，請就亨。」

translations.literal: Now Peng King already dead; your servant alive not like dead; request approach boiling.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Peng is already dead; I would rather die than live—send me to the cauldron.

sentences.zh: 上乃釋布，拜為都尉。

translations.literal: The sovereign then released Bu, appointed commandant.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor released him and appointed him commandant.

sentences.zh: 孝文時，為燕相，至將軍。

translations.literal: In Emperor Xiaowen's time, was Yan chancellor, reached general.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen he served as chancellor of Yan and rose to general.

sentences.zh: 布稱曰：「窮困不能辱身，非人也；

translations.literal: Bu declared saying: "Poor trapped yet cannot humble body—not a man;"
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bu used to say, "To be down and out yet refuse to demean oneself is not to be human;"

sentences.zh: 富貴不能快意，非賢也。」

translations.literal: Wealth honor yet cannot satisfy will—not worthy.
translations.idiomatic: to be rich and honored yet unable to follow one's conscience is not to be worthy.

sentences.zh: 於是嘗有德，厚報之；

translations.literal: Thus once had received virtue, thickly repaid it;
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, those who had once done him a kindness he repaid handsomely;

sentences.zh: 有怨，必以法滅之。

translations.literal: had grudge, surely by law destroyed them.
translations.idiomatic: those who had wronged him he was sure to destroy within the law.

sentences.zh: 吳楚反時，以功封為鄃侯，復為燕相。

translations.literal: When Wu-Chu rebelled, by merit enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu, again was Yan chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: During the rebellion of Wu and Chu he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu for his service and again became chancellor of Yan.

sentences.zh: 燕齊之間皆為立社，號曰欒公社。

translations.literal: Between Yan and Qi all for him established altars, called Luan Duke's altars.
translations.idiomatic: Between Yan and Qi the people founded shrines to him and called them the altars of Duke Luan.

sentences.zh: 布薨，子賁嗣侯，孝武時坐為太常犧牲不如令，國除。

translations.literal: Bu died; son Ben succeeded marquis; in Emperor Xiaowu time sat crime as Grand Master of Ceremonies sacrificial victims not according order; state removed.
translations.idiomatic: When Luan Bu died, his son Ben inherited the marquisate; under Emperor Wu he was convicted because sacrificial animals under his charge as Grand Master of Ceremonies failed to meet regulations, and the fief was abolished.

sentences.zh: 田叔，趙陘城人也。

translations.literal: Tian Shu, Zhao Xingcheng person.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu was a native of Xingcheng in Zhao.

sentences.zh: 其先，齊田氏也。

translations.literal: His ancestors, Qi Tian clan.
translations.idiomatic: His ancestors were the Tian clan of Qi.

sentences.zh: 叔好劍，學黃老術於樂鉅公。

translations.literal: Shu liked sword; studied Huang-Lao arts with Yue Ju-gong.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu loved the sword and studied the teachings of Huangdi and Laozi under Master Yue Ju.

sentences.zh: 為人廉直，喜任俠。

translations.literal: As man incorrupt straight, delighted in knight-errantry.
translations.idiomatic: He was incorruptible and upright by nature and delighted in knight-errantry.

sentences.zh: 游諸公，趙人舉之趙相趙午，言之趙王張敖，以為郎中。

translations.literal: Roamed the great lords; Zhao men raised him to Zhao chancellor Zhao Wu, spoke him to Zhao King Zhang Ao, made Gentleman of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: He moved among the great houses until men of Zhao recommended him to the Zhao chancellor Zhao Wu, who spoke of him to King Zhang Ao of Zhao, who appointed him Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 數歲，趙王賢之，未及遷。

translations.literal: Several years, Zhao king deemed him worthy, not yet reached promotion.
translations.idiomatic: For several years the king of Zhao admired him but had not yet promoted him.

sentences.zh: 會趙午、貫高等謀弒上，事發覺，漢下詔捕趙王及群臣反者。

translations.literal: It happened Zhao Wu, Guan Gao and others plotted assassinate the sovereign; affair discovered; Han issued edict arrest Zhao king and ministers who rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: It happened that Zhao Wu, Guan Gao, and others plotted to murder the emperor; when the plot was uncovered, the Han issued an edict to seize the king of Zhao and all ministers involved in the conspiracy.

sentences.zh: 趙有敢隨王，罪三族。

translations.literal: Zhao had whoever dared follow king, crime three clans.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhao, anyone who dared follow the king would suffer extermination of three clans.

sentences.zh: 唯田叔、孟舒等十餘人赭衣自髡鉗，隨王至長安。

translations.literal: Only Tian Shu, Meng Shu and more than ten others in crimson clothes themselves shaved-head cangue, followed the king to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Only Tian Shu, Meng Shu, and a dozen others shaved their heads, donned the crimson convict garb and iron collar, and followed their king to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 趙王敖事白，得出，廢王為宣平侯，乃進言叔等十人。

translations.literal: King Ao's of Zhao affair cleared, able to come out; demoted king to Marquis of Xuanping; then advanced speech Shu and ten men.
translations.idiomatic: When King Zhang Ao's case was cleared, he was released, stripped of his kingship, and enfeoffed as Marquis of Xuanping; he then recommended Tian Shu and the other ten men.

sentences.zh: 上召見，與語，漢廷臣無能出其右者。

translations.literal: The sovereign summoned and saw them, spoke with them; Han court ministers none able to come out their right.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor received them in audience and spoke with them, and no minister at the Han court proved their superior.

sentences.zh: 上說，盡拜為郡守、諸侯相。

translations.literal: The sovereign pleased, all appointed as governors and feudal chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: Pleased, the emperor appointed them all governors or chancellors to feudal kings.

sentences.zh: 叔為漢中守十餘年。

translations.literal: Shu was Hanzhong governor more than ten years.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu served as governor of Hanzhong for more than ten years.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝初立，召叔問曰：「公知天下長者乎？」

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen first ascended, summoned Shu and asked: "Sir, know the realm's elder-worthies?"
translations.idiomatic: Soon after Emperor Xiaowen came to the throne, he summoned Tian Shu and asked, "Do you know who in the realm are true men of honor?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「臣何足以知之！」

translations.literal: Replied saying: "Your servant how sufficient to know them!"
translations.idiomatic: He replied, "How could your servant be fit to judge such men!"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「公長者，宜知之。」

translations.literal: The sovereign said: "Sir is an elder-worthy; ought to know them."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "You are a man of honor yourself; you ought to know."

sentences.zh: 叔頓首曰：「故雲中守孟舒，長者也。」

translations.literal: Shu knocked head saying: "Former Yunzhong governor Meng Shu—an elder-worthy."
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu kowtowed and said, "The former governor of Yunzhong, Meng Shu, is such a man."

sentences.zh: 是時孟舒坐虜大入雲中免。

translations.literal: At this time Meng Shu sat crime: barbarians greatly entered Yunzhong, dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Meng Shu had been removed from office because the barbarians had raided Yunzhong in force.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「先帝置孟舒雲中十餘年矣，虜常一入，孟舒不能堅守，無故士卒戰死者數百人。

translations.literal: The sovereign said: "The late emperor placed Meng Shu at Yunzhong more than ten years; barbarians once entered, Meng Shu could not firmly defend; without reason soldiers who died in battle several hundred persons."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "The late emperor kept Meng Shu at Yunzhong for more than ten years, yet when the barbarians broke in he could not hold the line, and for no good reason several hundred soldiers died in the fighting."

sentences.zh: 長者固殺人乎？」

translations.literal: Elder-worthies thus kill men?
translations.idiomatic: Is that how a man of honor kills his own men?

sentences.zh: 叔叩頭曰：「夫貫高等謀反，天子下明詔，趙有敢隨張王者罪三族，然孟舒自髡鉗，隨張王，以身死之，豈自知為雲中守哉！

translations.literal: Shu knocked head saying: "As for Guan Gao and others plotting rebellion, the Son of Heaven issued clear edict: Zhao whoever dared follow King Zhang, crime three clans—yet Meng Shu himself shaved-head cangue, followed King Zhang, with body died for him—how could he have known he would become Yunzhong governor!"
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu kowtowed and said, "When Guan Gao and the others plotted treason, the Son of Heaven issued a clear edict that anyone in Zhao who dared follow King Zhang would suffer extermination of three clans; yet Meng Shu shaved his head and put on the collar of a convict and followed King Zhang, ready to die for him—how could he have known he would one day be governor of Yunzhong!"

sentences.zh: 漢與楚相距，士卒罷敝，而匈奴冒頓新服北夷，來為邊寇，孟舒知士卒罷敝，不忍出言，士爭臨城死敵，如子為父，以故死者數百人，孟舒豈敺之哉！

translations.literal: Han and Chu opposed each other; soldiers weary exhausted; while Xiongnu Modu newly subdued north barbarians, came as border raiders; Meng Shu knew soldiers weary exhausted, could not bear to speak order; knights vied to climb walls die against enemy, like sons for father; by this reason dead several hundred—how could Meng Shu have driven them!
translations.idiomatic: Han and Chu were locked in stalemate and the troops were worn out, while Modu of the Xiongnu had just brought the northern tribes to heel and turned them against our borders. Meng Shu saw how spent his men were and could not bring himself to give the harsh order; the soldiers vied to man the walls and die against the foe like sons for a father, and so several hundred perished—surely Meng Shu did not drive them to it!

sentences.zh: 是乃孟舒所以為長者。」

translations.literal: This then is Meng Shu the reason he is elder-worthy.
translations.idiomatic: That is precisely why Meng Shu is a true man of honor.

sentences.zh: 於是上曰：「賢哉孟舒！」

translations.literal: Thereupon the sovereign said: "Worthy indeed Meng Shu!"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor exclaimed, "Meng Shu is a worthy man indeed!"

sentences.zh: 復召以為雲中守。

translations.literal: Again summoned, made Yunzhong governor.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned him again and appointed him governor of Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 後數歲，叔坐法失官。

translations.literal: After several years, Shu by law sat, lost office.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later Tian Shu lost his post for a legal infraction.

sentences.zh: 梁孝王使人殺漢議臣爰盎，景帝召叔案梁，具得其事。

translations.literal: King Xiao of Liang sent men to kill Han deliberating minister Yuan Ang; Emperor Jing summoned Shu to investigate Liang, fully obtained the affair.
translations.idiomatic: King Xiao of Liang had had the Han counselor Yuan Ang murdered; Emperor Jing summoned Tian Shu to investigate the kingdom of Liang, and he uncovered the whole affair.

sentences.zh: 還報，上曰：「梁有之乎？」

translations.literal: Returned and reported; the sovereign said: "Liang had it?"
translations.idiomatic: When he returned and reported, the emperor asked, "Did Liang do it?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「有之。」

translations.literal: Replied saying: "There is it."
translations.idiomatic: He answered, "It did."

sentences.zh: 「事安在？」

translations.literal: "Affair where is it?"
translations.idiomatic: "Where is the proof?"

sentences.zh: 叔曰：「上無以梁事為問也。

translations.literal: Shu said: "The sovereign has nothing by Liang affair as question."
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu said, "Your Majesty should not press the matter of Liang."

sentences.zh: 今梁王不伏誅，是廢漢法也；

translations.literal: Now if Liang king does not submit to execution, this is abolishing Han law;
translations.idiomatic: If the king of Liang is not made to answer with his life, the laws of Han are set aside;

sentences.zh: 如其伏誅，太后食不甘味，臥不安席，此憂在陛下。」

translations.literal: if he submits to execution, empress dowager eats not sweet taste, lies not peaceful mat—this worry lies on Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: if he is executed, the empress dowager will lose her appetite and sleep—the burden of that grief will fall on Your Majesty.

sentences.zh: 於是上大賢之，以為魯相。

translations.literal: Thereupon the sovereign greatly deemed him worthy, made him Lu chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired him greatly and appointed him chancellor to the king of Lu.

sentences.zh: 相初至官，民以王取其財物自言者百餘人。

translations.literal: When chancellor first arrived at office, commoners because king took their goods and property self-spoke were more than hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: When the chancellor first took up his post, more than a hundred commoners came forward claiming that the king had seized their property.

sentences.zh: 叔取其渠率二千人笞，怒之曰：「王非汝主邪？

translations.literal: Shu took their channel-leaders two thousand men, flogged them, angrily said: "The king is not your lord?"
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu had the ringleaders among them—some two thousand men—flogged and rebuked them: "Is the king not your sovereign?"

sentences.zh: 何敢自言主！」

translations.literal: How dare self-speak of lord!
translations.idiomatic: You would dare denounce your sovereign in public!

sentences.zh: 魯王聞之，大慚，發中府錢，使相償之。

translations.literal: King of Lu heard it, greatly ashamed, issued central treasury cash, ordered chancellor repay them.
translations.idiomatic: When the king of Lu heard of this, he was deeply ashamed; he drew funds from the inner treasury and told the chancellor to make restitution.

sentences.zh: 相曰：「王自使人償之，不爾，是王為惡而相為善也。」

translations.literal: The chancellor said: "Let the king himself send men repay them; if not thus, this is king doing evil while chancellor does good."
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor replied, "Let the king send his own men to repay them; otherwise it will look as though the king did wrong and the chancellor played the benefactor."

sentences.zh: 魯王好獵，相常從入苑中，王輒休相就館。

translations.literal: King of Lu liked hunting; chancellor often followed into park middle; king then rested chancellor to go to lodge.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Lu loved the chase, and the chancellor often accompanied him into the hunting park; the king would send the chancellor off to the lodge to rest.

sentences.zh: 相常暴坐苑外，終不休，曰：「吾王暴露，獨何為舍？」

translations.literal: Chancellor often exposed sat outside park, to end not rested, said: "Our king is exposed to wind and sun—why alone should I take shelter?"
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor would sit in the open outside the park and refuse to go in, saying, "My king endures the elements; why should I alone take shelter?"

sentences.zh: 王以故不大出遊。

translations.literal: The king by this reason not greatly go out roam.
translations.idiomatic: For this reason the king seldom went out on long hunts.

sentences.zh: 數年以官卒，魯以百金祠，少子仁不受，曰：「義不傷先人名。」

translations.literal: Several years by office died; Lu with hundred gold sacrificed; youngest son Ren did not accept, said: "By righteousness not harm forebear's name."
translations.idiomatic: Some years later he died in office; Lu offered a hundred catties of gold for his rites, but his youngest son Ren refused it, saying, "Duty does not permit me to tarnish my father's name."

sentences.zh: 仁以壯勇為衛將軍舍人，數從擊匈奴。

translations.literal: Ren by stalwart courage was General of the Guards' retainer, repeatedly followed striking Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Ren, known for his vigor and courage, became a retainer of the General of the Guards and repeatedly accompanied him on campaigns against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 衛將軍進言仁為郎中，至二千石、丞相長史，失官。

translations.literal: General of the Guards advanced speech Ren as Gentleman of the Palace, reached two thousand piculs, chancellor chief clerk, lost office.
translations.idiomatic: The General of the Guards recommended him for Gentleman of the Palace; he rose to two-thousand-dan rank and chief clerk to the chancellor, then lost his post.

sentences.zh: 後使刺三河，還，奏事稱意，拜為京輔都尉。

translations.literal: Later sent inspector Sanhe, returned, reported affairs matched intent, appointed Metropolitan Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was sent to inspect the Three He commanderies; on his return his report pleased the emperor, and he was appointed Metropolitan Commandant.

sentences.zh: 月餘，遷司直。

translations.literal: After more than a month, promoted Director of Integrity.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a month later he was promoted to Director of Integrity.

sentences.zh: 數歲，戾太子舉兵，仁部閉城門，令太子得亡，坐縱反者族。

translations.literal: Several years, Crown Prince Ju raised troops; Ren's unit closed the city gates, allowing the heir to flee; convicted as one who released rebels, clan exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later, when Crown Prince Ju rose in revolt, Tian Ren's men manipulated the city gates so that the heir could escape; he was convicted of abetting rebels and his entire clan was put to death.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：以項羽之氣，而季布以勇顯名楚，身履軍搴旗者數矣，可謂壯士。

translations.literal: Appraisal says: With Xiang Yu's mettle in the land, Ji Bu by courage made a name in Chu; personally entered battle and seized enemy banners many times—may be called a stalwart.
translations.idiomatic: The historian writes: In an age dominated by Xiang Yu's fire, Ji Bu won renown in Chu for his courage; again and again he marched into battle and tore the enemy's banners from their poles—here was a true fighting man.

sentences.zh: 及至困厄奴僇，苟活而不變，何也？

translations.literal: Reaching to distress enslavement humiliation, shamefully alive yet not changed—why?
translations.idiomatic: Yet when reduced to bondage and humiliation he clung to life without wavering—why?

sentences.zh: 彼自負其材，受辱不羞，欲有所用其未足也，故終為漢名將。

translations.literal: He himself bore his talent, received shame not ashamed, wished to have something to use his not yet sufficient; therefore finally became Han famed general.
translations.idiomatic: He trusted in his own abilities and did not count disgrace a disgrace, for he still hoped to put his powers to use; that is why he ended his career as a celebrated general of Han.

sentences.zh: 賢者誠重其死。

translations.literal: Worthy ones truly weigh heavy their death.
translations.idiomatic: The wise truly weigh the value of their lives.

sentences.zh: 夫婢妾賤人，感概而自殺，非能勇也，其畫無俚之至耳。

translations.literal: As for maids concubines base persons, stirred indignation and self-killed—not able courage; their plan vulgarity utmost only.
translations.idiomatic: Maids, concubines, and other mean folk who take umbrage and cut their own throats are not displaying courage; their judgment is simply as shallow as can be.

sentences.zh: 欒布哭彭越，田叔隨張敖，赴死如歸，彼誠知所處，雖古烈士，何以加哉！

translations.literal: Luan Bu wept Peng Yue; Tian Shu followed Zhang Ao; went to death like returning—they truly knew where they stood; though ancient ardent martyrs, what could add!
translations.idiomatic: Luan Bu wept for Peng Yue; Tian Shu followed Zhang Ao to the grave as cheerfully as if going home—they knew exactly where they stood; even the sternest heroes of old could scarcely surpass them!
